source.author: SSA Catalog
source.notes: Crisp, tangy refrigerator pickles for shawarma wraps and bowls (turnips/cucumber/onion optional).

ingredients.label: Turnips, peeled and cut into sticks
notes: Classic shawarma-style pickles often include turnips.
Sub: radish or extra cucumber if you dislike turnip.

ingredients.label: Cucumber, cut into spears or coins
notes: Persian or English cucumber preferred for crunch.

ingredients.label: Carrot, cut into matchsticks
notes: Optional but adds sweetness and color.

ingredients.label: Beet slice or small beet piece
notes: Optional but traditional: adds pink color (like pickled turnips served with shawarma).
Remove after 24–48 hours once desired color is reached.

ingredients.label: White vinegar
notes: Sub: distilled vinegar or white wine vinegar for a softer tang.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: About 2 teaspoons. Reduce slightly if using fine salt.

ingredients.label: Sugar
notes: About 1 tablespoon; balances acidity. Optional but recommended.

ingredients.label: Coriander seeds
notes: About 1 teaspoon; optional but very shawarma-friendly.

steps.title: Prep the vegetables
instructions: Wash and prep vegetables: peel turnips; cut turnips and carrots into sticks; slice cucumber; thinly slice red onion.
Pack vegetables tightly into a clean quart jar (or two pint jars). Add garlic and optional beet piece among the vegetables.

steps.title: Make the brine
instructions: In a small saucepan (or heat-safe bowl for microwave), combine vinegar, water, salt, sugar, peppercorns, mustard seed, coriander seed, bay leaf, and optional chili flakes.
Warm just until salt and sugar dissolve (do not boil). Stir well.

steps.title: Pour and submerge
instructions: Carefully pour the warm brine over the vegetables until fully covered.
Tap the jar gently to release air bubbles. Ensure vegetables are submerged (use a small weight if needed).

steps.title: Cool, then refrigerate
instructions: Let cool to room temperature, then cap and refrigerate.
Quick-pickle timing: lightly pickled in 2–4 hours, best crunch/flavor after 24 hours.

steps.title: Color control (if using beet)
instructions: If using beet for pink color, check color after 24 hours.
Remove the beet when the desired color is reached to prevent earthy beet flavor from dominating.

steps.title: Serve and store
instructions: Use as a topping for shawarma wraps, bowls, and plates.
Store refrigerated and submerged in brine. Best within 7–10 days for crunch; usable up to 2–3 weeks if kept cold and clean.
